title: Executive Order 7264—Revocation of Executive Order No. 5807, of February 25, 1932, Withdrawing Public Lands, Colorado

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Revokes EO 5807 (1932 Colorado land withdrawal), effective upon official filing of the resurvey plat. Revocation is a self-executing status reversal keyed to a purely ministerial condition (plat filing); no discretionary authority is pooled in any official — the order does not delegate ongoing judgment to the Secretary of the Interior, it simply lifts a prior restriction on a defined trigger.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Nothing remains to fail: the revocation either takes effect automatically on plat-filing or does not. There is no implementation step requiring a responsible official's ongoing judgment, so no accountability gap can arise from this text.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Diff-aware check against the named predecessor: the predecessor order withdrew land pending resurvey (a temporary, condition-bound restriction); this order lifts that restriction on the resurvey's completion, restoring default public-land status rather than removing any oversight mechanism. No existing check (judicial, administrative, or otherwise) is eliminated.

title: Executive Order 7266—Modification of Executive Order No. 6910, of November 26, 1934, as Amended, Withdrawing Public Lands in Certain States, Wyoming

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Modifies EO 6910's blanket Taylor Grazing Act withdrawal to let the Secretary of the Interior withdraw one specifically described 320-acre Wyoming tract for use as a stock driveway under section 10 of the 1916 Act as amended. The order's own text already fixes the tract, acreage, and statutory purpose; Interior's remaining role is ministerial execution of an already-bounded designation, not open discretion.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Because the designation (tract, acreage, purpose) is fixed in the order itself, responsibility for the stock-driveway administration rests unambiguously with the Interior Department; there is no diffusion of accountability.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Diff-aware check against EO 6910 (the named predecessor, read in full): EO 6910 is itself a 'temporary withdrawal ... for classification and other purposes' under the Taylor Grazing Act, already amended and re-amended multiple times (this batch alone contains further amendments and outright revocations of sibling 1930s-era withdrawals under the same authority — e.g. EO 7264, 7269, 7271, 7272, 7275). This is a standing procedural/administrative land-classification framework that this project's own corpus shows is actively revised through ordinary supersession, not left frozen — the second evergreen category under the Flag 6 permanence carve-out. Scored ABSENT on that basis rather than the general 'continues until revoked' default.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight mechanism is removed; the order operates entirely within the same Pickett Act/Taylor Grazing Act framework as its predecessor.

title: Executive Order 7265—Modification of Executive Order of December 11, 1912, Withdrawing Public Lands as Power-Site Reserve No. 322, California

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Permits the State of California to use a specific 28.18-acre tract (already reserved as Power-Site Reserve No. 322) for cooperative forest-protection work, conditioned on the State vacating and removing improvements within 60 days of notice from the Secretary of the Interior. Interior's authority is bounded to administering this single, already-defined conditional arrangement.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: The order specifies exactly who must act and when: Interior gives notice when the land is needed for its reserved power/irrigation purpose, and the State must vacate within 60 days. This is an unusually clearly assigned accountability chain for an order this short.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: This does create a genuine, if narrow, ongoing compliance condition on the State of California (vacate and remove improvements within 60 days of notice). The trigger (Interior's notice) and the consequence (vacate/removal) are both stated in specific, unambiguous terms, so there is no vagueness of the kind Flag 4 targets.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: No perverse incentive is created: the removal condition does not reward the State for delay or non-compliance, and the underlying power-site reservation is preserved regardless of how the temporary cooperative use plays out.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Unlike the sibling tract orders in this batch, this order carries its own explicit, self-contained termination trigger in the text itself (60 days after notice that the land is needed) rather than relying on the general Taylor-Grazing-Act supersession pattern used to justify ABSENT elsewhere in this batch — a stronger and more direct basis for the same conclusion.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight mechanism is removed; the power-site reservation's primary purpose is expressly preserved and takes priority over the new cooperative use.

flags.name: Third-Party Incentive Gaps
flags.justification: Third-party standing is unnecessary here because the agency itself (Interior) is the party with the direct interest in timely vacatur once the land is needed for its reserved purpose — enforcement does not depend on the regulated party's (California's) self-reporting.

title: Executive Order 7262—Amendment of Executive Order No. 3206, of December 30, 1919, Establishing the Board of Surveys and Maps

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Re-establishes the Federal Board of Surveys and Maps as a 24-agency coordinating body whose members are appointed by their own agency heads and serve without compensation. The Board's function is explicitly recommendatory ('shall make recommendations') and informational (a central data-clearinghouse); no decision authority is pooled anywhere — each member agency retains its own mapping/surveying authority untouched.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Because the Board has no operative authority to fail — it coordinates and recommends, but implements nothing itself — responsibility for any mapping or surveying failure remains with the individual agency that owns that function, not with the Board.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: The order's own text names its full amendment lineage (EOs 3246, 3391, 4547, 5268, 6918, spanning 1920-1934) before adding this sixth amendment — direct, explicit textual evidence that this standing coordinating body is maintained through a documented, recurring pattern of periodic amendment rather than left frozen. This is the clearest instance in the batch of the 'standing procedural framework' carve-out being satisfied by the order's own text rather than by inference from sibling orders.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed; if anything the order broadens a voluntary coordination mechanism ('requested to make full use of the Board').

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: No significant downstream precedent identified beyond routine inter-agency coordination: the Board's role is advisory and informational only, membership is voluntary, and departments are only 'requested,' not required, to use it.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: The Board implicates essentially every major department and several independent agencies (War, Commerce, Interior, Agriculture, Navy, State, TVA, the Federal Power Commission, the Library of Congress, among others), so this is genuinely answerable rather than NOT_APPLICABLE. But the coordination is clean: no member agency's existing statutory mapping or surveying authority is reassigned, overridden, or redirected — the Board only recommends and centralizes information, and participation is voluntary.

title: Executive Order 7263—Designating the Secretary of the Treasury to Act in Respect of Interest or Claims of the United States

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Designates the Secretary of the Treasury as the official who represents the federal government's financial 'interest or claims' in section 77(e) railroad-reorganization bankruptcy proceedings. The underlying claims themselves remain subject to ordinary bankruptcy-court adjudication, which is an external check on how those claims are resolved; Treasury's role is representational, not an unchecked new grant of substantive authority.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Treasury is now unambiguously the responsible official for representing these federal claims; there is no diffusion, since the order names a single, specific office rather than leaving the function unassigned or shared.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: This is a routine, permanent designation of which official performs an ongoing representational function (the government will continually have financial interests in bankruptcy proceedings); the designated function is administrative/procedural in nature rather than a discretionary or emergency grant, fitting the standing-administrative-function carve-out.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed; ordinary bankruptcy court process, which is the operative check on the claims themselves, is untouched by this designation.

title: Executive Order 7268—Designation of Coast Guard Vessels for Special Winter Cruising

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Directs the Secretary of the Treasury/Coast Guard Commandant to assign vessels for winter distress-rescue cruising under an existing 19th-century statutory Coast Guard function (Rev. Stat. sec. 1536). Discretion over which vessels and how many is operational/ministerial (fleet deployment), not a new unchecked grant of authority over any outside party.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Responsibility for executing the seasonal patrol runs through the existing, well-defined Coast Guard chain of command (Secretary of the Treasury to the Commandant); no diffusion is created.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: This is a standing seasonal operational directive under a permanent, evergreen safety-of-navigation mission with no natural end-state — winter distress-rescue capacity does not lapse or need periodic reauthorization the way emergency or crisis-specific authority would.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight mechanism is removed by this order.

title: Executive Order 7267—Modification of Executive Order No. 6910, of November 26, 1934, as Amended, Withdrawing Public Lands in Certain States

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Modifies EO 6910 to let Interior withdraw specifically described Montana, Nevada, and Wyoming tracts for Department of Commerce air-navigation facilities under section 4 of the Air Commerce Act of 1928. As with EO 7266, the tracts and statutory purpose are fixed in the order's own text; Interior's role is ministerial execution of an already-bounded designation.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Responsibility for the designated tracts rests clearly with Interior (withdrawal) and Commerce (facility use); no diffusion of accountability is created.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Same basis as EO 7266: a tract-specific carve-out from the actively and repeatedly amended EO 6910 Taylor Grazing Act framework, which this corpus shows is revised through ordinary supersession rather than left frozen.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight mechanism is removed.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Genuinely implicates two agencies (Interior withdrawing, Commerce using the land for air-navigation aids under an explicit joint statutory scheme), so this is answerable rather than NOT_APPLICABLE — but the coordination is clean: Commerce's existing statutory air-navigation authority is not redirected or countermanded, it is simply given a physical site to exercise authority it already held.

title: Executive Order 7270—Withdrawal of Public Lands for Lookout Stations, Oregon

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Section 1 revokes the prior blanket withdrawal as to specific Oregon tracts (self-executing, no new authority created — same reasoning as the revocation-template orders in this batch). Section 2 re-withdraws those same tracts for Forest Service fire-lookout-station use, a narrow, already-bounded administrative purpose; Section 3's 'continues... unless and until revoked' language is the ordinary boilerplate default rather than evidence of unchecked discretion, since no official is given open-ended judgment beyond administering the named tracts for the named purpose.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Responsibility for the lookout-station tracts rests clearly with the Forest Service under the Siskiyou National Forest administration named in the text.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: This is the first order in the batch to state the 'continues... unless and until revoked' clause explicitly (Sec. 3) rather than relying on the general Taylor Grazing Act framework as sibling orders EO 7266/7267 do. Judgment call, logged explicitly: fire-lookout station land, like the stock-driveway and air-navigation-facility carve-outs, is an ongoing operational/administrative land dedication rather than emergency or crisis-response authority, so it does not have the kind of natural end-state that reauthorization pressure would track. Scored on the same substantive basis as the other tract designations in this batch, notwithstanding that this is the first to spell out the default boilerplate directly — the presence or absence of that boilerplate phrase is not itself treated as dispositive.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed; Section 1's revocation restores default status rather than eliminating a check, and Section 2 does not touch any existing review mechanism.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Implicates two agencies (Interior withdrawing, Forest Service/Agriculture administering the lookout stations), so this is answerable — the coordination is clean, assigning Agriculture a physical site for a fire-management function it already administers under existing forest law, not redirecting or countermanding anyone's mandate.

title: Executive Order 7274—Amendment of Executive Order No. 6910, of November 26, 1934, as Amended, Withdrawing Public Land in Certain States

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrows EO 6910's blanket withdrawal by excluding lands already within duly-established Taylor Grazing Act grazing districts. This releases land from the broader withdrawal back into the Act's own defined grazing-district administration; it reduces rather than concentrates authority.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: No diffusion is created; released lands revert to grazing-district administration under the 1934 Act's own existing accountability structure.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: This order removes coverage rather than creating any new ongoing grant of authority — diff-aware comparison against EO 6910 shows a narrowing amendment, not an extension, so there is no new authority for this flag to assess; scored NOT_APPLICABLE would also be defensible, but ABSENT is used here since the underlying grazing-district administration these lands revert to is itself a standing statutory framework with its own accountability structure, not an open-ended grant.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed; arguably the opposite — lands revert from a general blanket-withdrawal regime to the more specific, statutorily defined grazing-district administration.

title: Executive Order 7273—Exemption of Benjamin W. Kumler from Compulsory Retirement for Age

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Exercises a narrow, individually-named, explicitly time-bound exemption (one person, one civil-service position, continued service only 'until February 1, 1937') under the exact statutory exception Congress itself wrote (act of June 30, 1932, sec. 204). The President's discretion here is the specific, bounded mechanism the statute created, not an open-ended concentration of power.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Single named individual, single bounded term; no diffusion of responsibility is possible.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: This is a clean contrast case against EO 11154 (Hoover's indefinite retirement exemption, later in the corpus, named in this project's findings log as the most structurally significant individual-exemption order found): this exemption carries an explicit, bounded end date (February 1, 1937) rather than an indefinite term, requiring an affirmative renewal decision if continued service is still wanted. The bounded-term pattern matches the other individual retirement exemptions already logged in this project (EO 7525, 8047, 8231, 8287, 9033) rather than the Hoover outlier.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed; the statutory retirement-age safeguard itself remains intact and simply resumes applying to Kumler at the stated date absent further action.

title: Executive Order 7277—Designation of Under Secretary, First Assistant Secretary, and Assistant Secretary of the Interior to Act as Secretary of the Interior

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Establishes a fixed, statutorily-grounded (Rev. Stat. sec. 179) order of succession for who acts as Secretary of the Interior during absence/illness. This clarifies rather than concentrates authority — it specifies which existing office exercises the Secretary's authority temporarily, using a defined priority chain, not open discretion.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: The explicit priority order (Under Secretary, then First Assistant Secretary, then Assistant Secretary) leaves no ambiguity about who is responsible at any given moment.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: The order's own text states 'This order supersedes Executive Order No. 7198 of September 26, 1935' — an explicit, direct supersession clause, exactly the textual evidence the standing-procedural-framework carve-out calls for. This is a routine, ordinarily-revised administrative succession designation, not a discretionary or emergency grant.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight

title: Executive Order 7276—Authorizing the Appointment of Lewis R. Barrett to the Position of Recreation Coordinator, National Park Service, Department of the Interior, Without Regard to Civil-Service Rules

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, single-position appointment authorization; the President's discretion under the Civil Service Act's public-interest exception is bounded to this one named individual and position, not a general or ongoing grant of hiring authority.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Single named position and appointee; no diffusion of responsibility for the appointment itself.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: Direct textual analog to this project's own EO 8451 precedent (a 90-word civil-service-rules waiver later found to fire this flag on reconciliation): the order appoints Barrett 'without compliance with the requirements of the civil-service rules and without being accorded a classified status.' This bypasses competitive merit-selection for the position, creating a structural incentive toward patronage-based appointment over merit competition — the same mechanism previously identified in this project, not a fresh interpretation.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Unlike the bounded-term individual exemptions coded elsewhere in this batch (e.g. EO 7273, 7278: both expire on a stated date and require affirmative renewal), this order sets no term at all — Barrett's unclassified status persists indefinitely with no review point. This is a smaller-scale echo of the indefinite-exemption structure this project's own findings log identifies as its most severe individual-exemption pattern (EO 11154, Hoover) — an unreviewed, open-ended removal of a structural safeguard (competitive/classified status).

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Same textual basis as Flag 5: civil-service competitive selection is a pre-existing check on merit-based hiring, and the order explicitly disables it for this position ('without compliance with the requirements of the civil-service rules'). Following this project's own EO 8451 precedent for the identical bypass language.

title: Executive Order 7278—Exemption of William H. Ramsey from Compulsory Retirement for Age

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, individually-named, explicitly time-bound exemption (continued service only 'until February 1, 1937') under the same statutory mechanism as EO 7273 coded earlier in this batch.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Single named individual, single bounded term; no diffusion of responsibility is possible.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Bounded, explicit end date (February 1, 1937) requiring affirmative renewal — same clean pattern as EO 7273, contrasting with the indefinite-exemption structure named in this project's findings log (EO 11154).

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: The statutory retirement-age safeguard resumes applying to Ramsey at the stated date absent further action; nothing is permanently removed.

title: Executive Order 7280—Evidencing, Validating, and Confirming the Creation of the Federal Housing Administration

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A curative/ratifying order: it retroactively documents and confirms the FHA's creation and Administrator's appointment, both of which had already occurred in 1934 and had already been Senate-confirmed (Jan. 18, 1935). No new discretionary authority is created or concentrated by this text; it memorializes an already-completed, already-checked action.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: No new accountability question is raised — the FHA Administrator's accountability was already established under the National Housing Act before this order.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No existing oversight is removed; if anything the order exists specifically because the original 1934 creation's formal documentation was apparently incomplete, and this closes that gap rather than opening one.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Narrow, agency-specific curative act; the text does not claim or exercise any broader retroactive-ratification authority beyond confirming this one agency's already-completed creation, so no meaningful downstream precedent is identifiable from this text alone.

title: Executive Order 7281—Designating the Chairman of the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Designates Marriner Eccles as Chairman for a defined four-year term under the Banking Act of 1935's own statutory mechanism; the underlying Board seat itself is Senate-confirmed. No open-ended discretion is created.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Single named position, defined term, no diffusion of responsibility.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Explicit, bounded four-year term requiring renewal or redesignation — a clean example of built-in periodic reauthorization pressure.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No existing oversight is removed; the order simply exercises the statutory designation power Congress created.

title: Executive Order 7285—Prescribing the Official Flag of the Vice President of the United States

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Purely ceremonial/symbolic design specification; no operative authority over any person or agency is created.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Nothing to fail; a flag design has no implementation-failure mode.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No existing oversight mechanism is touched.

title: Executive Order 7286—Exemption of Charles Holzman from Compulsory Retirement for Age

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, individually-named, explicitly time-bound exemption ('until March 1, 1937'); same statutory mechanism as EO 7273/7278.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Single named individual, single bounded term.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Bounded, explicit end date requiring affirmative renewal — consistent with the clean pattern established by EO 7273/7278 in this same batch.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Statutory retirement-age safeguard resumes at the stated date absent further action.

title: Executive Order 7287—Selection of Members of the Central Statistical Board

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Establishes a distributed, partially self-perpetuating 13-member selection process (six members designated one each by six different department/agency heads; seven elected jointly by the Chairman and those six) rather than pooling selection authority in any single official. This is a deliberately distributed structure, not a concentration.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Membership terms, vacancy-filling timelines (within two months), and eligibility conditions (at least one elected member not a permanent federal employee) are all explicitly defined, leaving clear responsibility for maintaining a properly constituted Board.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: An unusually well-designed rotating-term structure: designated members serve at the pleasure of their appointing officer but not more than two years without redesignation, and elected members serve fixed two-year terms (one year for the initial 1936 cohort) requiring affirmative reelection. This is genuine built-in reauthorization pressure on Board composition, not an indefinite or self-perpetuating grant.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed; this creates a structured membership process where the underlying 1935 Act had left the mechanics unspecified.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Narrow implementation of an already-Congressionally-created board's membership mechanics; the text does not itself expand the Board's substantive statistical-coordination authority, so no independent second-order claim is identifiable beyond the board's already-legislated mandate.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Six different departments/agencies (Treasury, Interior, Agriculture, Commerce, Labor, plus the Federal Reserve Board Chairman) each designate one member — genuinely implicates multiple agencies, so this is answerable rather than NOT_APPLICABLE. The coordination is clean: each agency contributes one representative to a shared board with no redirection of any agency's existing statutory mandate.

title: Executive Order 7288—Transferring to the Control and Jurisdiction of the Treasury Department a Portion of the Site of the Mayaguez Barracks Military Reservation at Mayaguez, Puerto Rico

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow tract transfer between two federal departments (War to Treasury) for a specific, defined purpose (federal building); Interior's/Treasury's role is bounded to that stated purpose.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear responsibility, and an explicit reversion mechanism (control reverts to the War Department if the tract ceases to be used for federal-building purposes and the War Department is formally notified) leaves no accountability gap.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Explicit, textually-stated conditional reversion trigger tied to continued use for the stated purpose — a genuine, direct termination mechanism, not merely an inferred pattern.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No existing oversight is removed.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Implicates two federal departments (War, Treasury) in a jurisdiction transfer; genuinely answerable. The explicit, self-enforcing reversion clause protects the War Department's interest if Treasury's use ends, making this a clean, reciprocal transfer rather than a one-directional stripping of authority.

title: Executive Order 7289—Modification of Executive Order No. 6910, of November 26, 1934, as Amended, Withdrawing Public Lands in Certain States

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Modifies EO 6910 to permit Interior to withdraw a specifically described ~400-acre California tract for reclamation purposes under section 3 of the 1902 Reclamation Act; tract and purpose are fixed in the order's own text.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, bounded responsibility for the designated tract; no diffusion.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Same basis as EO 7266/7267/7289's sibling carve-outs earlier in this batch: a tract-specific designation from the actively and repeatedly amended EO 6910 framework, which this corpus shows is revised through ordinary supersession.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 7291—Exemption of Charles J. Evans from Compulsory Retirement For Age

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, individually-named, explicitly time-bound exemption ('until March 1, 1937'); same statutory mechanism as the other individual retirement exemptions in this batch.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Single named individual, single bounded term.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Bounded, explicit end date requiring affirmative renewal.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Statutory retirement-age safeguard resumes at the stated date absent further action.

title: Executive Order 7292—Establishing Lake Andes Migratory Waterfowl Refuge, South Dakota

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Implements a specific statutory conservation designation (Migratory Bird Conservation Act) administered by the Department of Agriculture over a bounded, described acreage; no unchecked discretionary authority is created.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Single responsible agency (Agriculture), narrow single-purpose designation; no diffusion.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Wildlife-refuge conservation designations are evergreen by nature — consistent with this project's own findings log, which already names the same pattern for sibling 1930s migratory-bird-refuge orders (EO 7678, 7801, 7882). There is no emergency/crisis character to this authority that reauthorization pressure would meaningfully track.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: The order explicitly preserves 'valid existing rights,' rather than displacing any existing check.

title: Executive Order 7293—Regulations Governing the Granting of Allowances for Quarters and Subsistence to Enlisted Men

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Implements a detailed, objective rate schedule under existing statutory authority (act of June 10, 1922, sec. 11); entitlements are fixed by table, not discretionary judgment.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Objective, tabular criteria leave clear, unambiguous responsibility for correct payment.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: This does create an ongoing administrative-payment relationship, so the flag is answerable rather than inapplicable — but the mechanics are exceptionally well-specified: exact dollar amounts by category and location, and precise day-counting rules (e.g., 'the day to begin at midnight,' fractional-day thresholds stated in hours). Two different administering officers could not reasonably reach different payment conclusions from this text.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: No perverse incentive is identifiable in the fixed-rate structure itself.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: The order's own text names its full six-order supersession chain (EOs 4728, 5022, 5256, 5288, 6605, 7224, spanning 1927-1935) before superseding them — direct textual evidence of the standing-procedural-framework carve-out, the same basis used for EO 7262 earlier in this batch.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No existing oversight is removed.

flags.name: Third-Party Incentive Gaps
flags.justification: Individual service members have direct personal access to and interest in their own pay records, which substitutes for the need for an external third-party watchdog of the kind Flag 8 otherwise looks for in diffuse regulatory schemes.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Applies uniformly across multiple services (Army, Navy, Marine Corps, Coast Guard, Coast and Geodetic Survey, Public Health Service) — genuinely implicates multiple agencies, so answerable rather than NOT_APPLICABLE. This is a shared, coordinated standard, not a jurisdictional conflict.

title: Executive Order 7294—Waiver of the Time Limit Provided in Civil-Service Rule IX to Permit the Reinstatement in the Postal Service of Mrs. Nell J. Kline

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, individually-named, compassionate waiver (widow of a postal inspector who died on official duty) of a specific procedural time limit, issued 'on the recommendation of the Postmaster General' — an internal endorsement requirement built directly into the order's own text.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Single named beneficiary, clear stated basis, agency recommendation on record.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: Distinguishable from EO 7276 coded earlier in this batch: this waiver restores Mrs. Kline to normal classified civil-service status through reinstatement, rather than exempting anyone from classified status on an ongoing basis. Because the direction is restorative (back into the merit system) rather than extractive (out of it, indefinitely), there is no comparable ongoing incentive-distortion mechanism, so this is properly NOT_APPLICABLE rather than PRESENT.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Civil-Service Rule IX's time limit is a safeguard; this order waives it once, for one person, on a compassionate basis, with the Postmaster General's recommendation on the record — a materially different, more checked structure than EO 7276's unilateral, unendorsed bypass earlier in this batch.

title: Executive Order 7295—Establishing Lake Bowdoin Migratory Waterfowl Refuge, Montana

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Implements the Migratory Bird Conservation Act over described Montana lands, administered by Agriculture, with an explicit shooting-area option operated only under Agriculture-approved cooperative agreement/regulation. Bounded, statutorily-anchored designation.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, bounded responsibility (Agriculture), explicit conditions on any shooting-area use.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Evergreen conservation designation, consistent with EO 7292 (this batch) and the sibling migratory-bird-refuge pattern already named in this project's findings log.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: The order explicitly subordinates the new refuge designation to the pre-existing Interior Department reclamation withdrawal ('subject to the use thereof by said Department for irrigation and other incidental purposes and to any other existing valid rights') — an affirmative non-interference clause protecting the prior use, not a removal of it.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Implicates two agencies (Agriculture's new refuge designation over land 'primarily under the jurisdiction of the Department of the Interior' for reclamation) — genuinely answerable. The explicit non-interference language is a real positive design feature: this predates, and matches, the pattern this project's findings log names for EO 7678/7801/7882 (later 1930s refuge orders sharing land with another agency's prior use) — worth flagging as an earlier instance of the same recurring convention.

title: Executive Order 7296—Amending Executive Order No. 7046, of May 20, 1935, Prescribing Rules and Regulations Relating to Wages, Hours of Work, and Conditions of Employment Under the Emergency Relief Appropriation Act of 1935

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The WPA Administrator's sole 'opinion' determines whether workers must make up lost time without additional compensation, with no external check on that judgment named in the text.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Because the Administrator both wields this discretion and would be the one to assess whether it was properly used, there is no separate mechanism for holding the exercise of this power accountable if misapplied.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'If, in the opinion of the Administrator, progress on the entire program or any part thereof will be expedited thereby' is a subjective standard with no defined criteria; two different administrators could reach substantially different conclusions about when unpaid makeup work is required.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: The clause creates an incentive for the implementing agency to invoke this standard broadly to extract additional uncompensated labor whenever convenient, framed as program expedition, from workers who have no defined recourse.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: This regulation operates only within the Emergency Relief Appropriation Act of 1935's own funding authority, which is itself subject to periodic Congressional reappropriation — an external, structural reauthorization pressure built into the underlying program's own annual-appropriations mechanism, distinguishing this from an open-ended emergency-powers grant with no natural check.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: This adds a new discretionary power rather than removing any pre-existing oversight mechanism; Flag 7 targets removal specifically, not the addition of an imperfect new power (which is captured under Flags 1, 4, and 5 instead).

flags.name: Third-Party Incentive Gaps
flags.justification: No external party (e.g., a labor board, inspector, or union representative) is named with standing to review whether the Administrator's opinion was reasonably invoked; the affected workers themselves are the only check, and they lack independent access to override the Administrator's judgment.

title: Executive Order 7297—To Provide for the Protection and Preservation of the Domestic Sources of Tin

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Delegates tin-scrap export licensing to the Secretary of State, but only 'by and with the advice and consent of the [National Munitions Control] Board' — a genuine, textually-required external check on the delegated authority, not a unilateral grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: The Board consent requirement gives clear, shared responsibility rather than pooling judgment in one official alone.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'Such conditions and regulations as he may find necessary to assure... fair and equitable consideration to all producers' is an undefined standard; two different licensing determinations could reasonably reach different views of what counts as fair and equitable treatment among competing tin producers.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: This creates a standing licensing regime under a newly-enacted statute with no stated expiration or review point, and — unlike the appropriations-bound WPA regulation in EO 7296 — nothing in this text ties its continuation to a periodic external check; absent stronger evidence of a natural end-state, the general default (fires when authority persists indefinitely with no review mechanism) applies.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed; the Board-consent requirement is, if anything, a check preserved and relied upon.

flags.name: Third-Party Incentive Gaps
flags.justification: Rival tin-scrap producers have both standing and direct incentive to scrutinize whether licensing is being administered 'fairly and equitably' to all producers, since favoritism toward a competitor directly harms their own interests — a natural third-party check inherent in the statute's own 'all producers' language.

title: Executive Order 7298—Regulations Governing the Preparation, Presentation, Filing, and Distribution of Executive Orders and Proclamations

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Establishes a sequential, multi-office approval chain (Bureau of the Budget, then Attorney General, then Federal Register Division) for all future executive orders and proclamations; no single office has unchecked final say short of the President himself, and a disapproval must be accompanied by written reasons if the order is to proceed further.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Each office's role (BOB for initial review, AG for 'form and legality,' Federal Register Division for technical/publication compliance) is clearly and separately defined.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: The disapproval-and-reasons procedure, and the specific formatting/citation requirements (paragraph 1), leave little room for inconsistent application.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: No incentive distortion is identifiable in this procedural chain.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: The order's own text states it 'shall thereupon supersede Executive Order No. 6247, of August 10, 1933' — an explicit supersession clause, the same category of direct textual evidence for the standing-procedural-framework carve-out used elsewhere in this batch (EO 7262, EO 7293).

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed; this order adds review steps rather than eliminating any.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: This order establishes the review architecture (Bureau of the Budget clearance, then Attorney General sign-off on 'form and legality,' then Federal Register certification) that would govern the preparation of essentially every subsequent executive order — a genuine institutional precedent persisting far beyond this order's own immediate administrative purpose, and the direct ancestor of the modern OMB/OLC executive-order clearance process.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Implicates the Bureau of the Budget, the Department of Justice, and the Division of the Federal Register/National Archives in a shared, coordinated procedure; no agency's existing mandate is redirected or countermanded by this coordination.

title: Executive Order 7299—Placing Kure (Ocean) Island, Territory of Hawaii, Under the Control and Jurisdiction of the Secretary of the Navy

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow jurisdictional placement of one remote Pacific island for naval purposes; no ongoing discretionary authority over any person or program is created.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Single responsible agency (Navy), narrow purpose.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: An administrative jurisdictional designation for a defined operational (naval) purpose, evergreen in the same sense as the batch's other land/jurisdiction designations, not an emergency or crisis-specific grant.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 7300—Exemption of Henry C. Stuart from Compulsory Retirement for Age

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, individually-named exemption extending a prior bounded exemption (EO 6932, one year) by a further explicit bounded term ('until March 1, 1937').

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Single named individual, single bounded term, clear predecessor cited.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Explicit, bounded end date requiring affirmative renewal — consistent with this batch's clean individual-exemption pattern (EO 7273, 7278, 7286), here extended a second time rather than made indefinite.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Statutory retirement-age safeguard resumes at the stated date absent further action.

title: Executive Order 7301—Enlarging Niobrara Reservation

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Implements the Migratory Bird Conservation Act over described Nebraska lands, administered by Agriculture, as an enlargement of an existing, named reservation (EO 1461/1642/3256) — bounded, statutorily-anchored, no unchecked discretion.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Single responsible agency, narrow single-purpose enlargement.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Evergreen wildlife-conservation designation, consistent with EO 7292/7295 (this batch) and the migratory-bird-refuge pattern already named in this project's findings log.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Explicitly preserves 'valid existing rights.'

title: Executive Order 7302—Transferring Certain Lands to the Control and Jurisdiction of the Secretary of the Navy, Virgin Islands

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, plat-bounded jurisdictional transfer (Interior to Navy) for a stated purpose (aviation facilities); the Navy officer's discretion to regulate the excepted recreational areas is limited to safety coordination 'during seaplane operations,' not general authority over those areas, which remain excepted from the transfer entirely.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear responsible agency for the transferred area; excepted areas remain outside Navy jurisdiction.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: An operational-purpose jurisdictional transfer, evergreen/administrative in the same sense as the batch's other land-transfer orders.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Explicitly preserves 'valid existing rights' and affirmatively excepts specific pre-existing recreational areas (the Lindbergh Bay beach/bath house and the golf house) from the transfer altogether, rather than displacing those uses — a genuinely protective design feature.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Implicates two agencies (Interior transferring, Navy receiving); the explicit carve-out of recreational areas from the transfer is a further instance of the non-interference-clause family already logged elsewhere in this batch (EO 7295) and in this project's findings log (EO 7678/7801/7882), here applied to protect civilian recreational use against a military jurisdictional transfer rather than one federal land use against another.

title: Executive Order 7303—Withdrawal of Public Land for Lookout Station, Oregon

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Same structure as EO 7270 earlier in this batch: Section 1 revokes prior blanket withdrawal as to a specific 40-acre tract (self-executing); Section 2 re-withdraws it for a narrow, bounded Forest Service fire-lookout purpose.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Responsibility rests clearly with the Forest Service under the named national forest's administration.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Same basis as EO 7270: an operational/administrative land dedication (fire-lookout station) with no natural end-state tied to an emergency or crisis, notwithstanding the 'continues... unless and until revoked' boilerplate in Section 3.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed; Section 1 restores default status rather than eliminating a check.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Implicates two agencies (Interior withdrawing, Forest Service/Agriculture administering); clean coordination, consistent with EO 7270.

title: Executive Order 7304—Appointment of Collectors of Cotton Statistics

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Extends an existing, long-standing civil-service schedule-A category for part-time/intermittent employment (compensation not exceeding $540/year) to Census Bureau cotton-statistics collectors. This is materially different from the individual full merit-bypass orders elsewhere in this batch (EO 7276, EO 7306): it places a class of positions within an established, general classification already 'existing for many years,' rather than creating an ad hoc unclassified appointment for one named person.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Applies a defined, pre-existing classification category rather than open-ended individual discretion.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Routine civil-service classification-schedule housekeeping, revised through ordinary amendment — the order itself amends a named 1933 predecessor (EO 6141), direct textual evidence for the standing-procedural-framework carve-out.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 7305—Allocating Funds to the Farm Credit Administration and Prescribing Rules and Regulations for the Making of Emergency Crop Loans Under the Emergency Relief Appropriation Act of 1935

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The FCA Governor is given broad discretion to set the terms and conditions for a nationwide, multi-million-dollar ($30,000,000) direct-lending program — eligibility conditions, security requirements, and administrative rules are all left to what 'the Governor may prescribe' — with no named external reviewer of that discretion.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: The Governor both sets the lending rules and administers the program under them; no separate body is named to hold that combined rule-setting-and-implementing role accountable if the program is administered unevenly or improperly.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: Each applicant must establish eligibility 'to the satisfaction of the proper officer,' an inherently subjective standard with no objective criteria specified; two loan officers could reach different conclusions about the same applicant's eligibility.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: Section 6(b) authorizes the Farm Credit Administration to 'appoint officers and employees without regard to the provisions of the Civil Service laws and regulations, and fix the compensation of any officers and employees so appointed without regard to the Classification Act of 1923' — a program-wide, not merely individual, civil-service and pay-scale bypass for the entire emergency-lending staff. This is the same structural mechanism as the EO 8451/EO 7276/EO 7306 civil-service-bypass pattern already identified in this project, but applied here at institutional scale rather than to one named person.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Unlike EO 7296's inferred appropriations-boundedness, this order states an explicit termination date directly in its own text: administrative-expense authority runs only 'to and including June 30, 1937' (Sec. 5) — a genuine, textually-stated sunset on the program's administrative funding.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight mechanism is shown to be removed by this text.

flags.name: Third-Party Incentive Gaps
flags.justification: No external party (e.g., an inspector general, a legislative oversight committee, or a borrower-advocacy mechanism) is named with standing to surface improper loan administration or hiring practices; borrowers themselves have limited incentive to challenge lenient treatment of themselves, and no other actor is given access to test the Governor's discretion.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Beyond the individual eligibility and lending-discretion concerns already captured above, the program-wide civil-service-and-classification-act bypass is itself a distinct institutional precedent: it establishes that an entire emergency program's staff can be hired and paid entirely outside the merit system, a pattern which — if replicated across the many other emergency-relief agencies of this era — would represent a meaningful, lasting erosion of the classified civil service's practical scope, independent of how any single loan is administered.

title: Executive Order 7306—Authorizing the Appointment of Mrs. Hilda McTeer to a Position in the Classified Service of the Government Without Regard to Civil-Service Rules

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, single-position appointment authorization for one named individual; the concern here is the merit-selection bypass itself (Flags 5 and 7), not a broader concentration of authority.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Single named position, clear stated basis (widow of a deceased Treasury employee), and a named recommending official.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: Direct match to the 'without compliance with the requirements of the civil-service rules' bypass language already identified in this project (EO 8451 precedent) and in EO 7276 earlier in this batch. Worth noting a partial mitigating factor absent from EO 7276: the order states it 'is recommended by the Secretary of the Treasury,' an internal endorsement on the record — but that recommendation does not restore competitive merit selection, so the underlying incentive concern (patronage appointment bypassing competition) still applies.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: No term is stated for this unclassified appointment; like EO 7276, this is indefinite rather than bounded, differing from this batch's bounded individual-exemption pattern.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Same textual basis as Flag 5: the civil-service competitive process is explicitly bypassed for this position, with the Secretary's recommendation partially, but not fully, offsetting the loss of that check.

title: Executive Order 7307—Exemption of Luther L. Browning from Compulsory Retirement for Age

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, individually-named exemption extending prior bounded exemptions (EO 6663, EO 6984) by a further explicit bounded term ('until November 1, 1936').

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Single named individual, single bounded term, clear predecessor chain cited.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Explicit, bounded end date requiring affirmative renewal, now the third such renewal for this individual — consistent with the batch's clean individual-exemption pattern.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Statutory retirement-age safeguard resumes at the stated date absent further action.

title: Executive Order 7308—Exemption of Carl F. Jeansen from Compulsory Retirement for Age

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, individually-named exemption extending a prior bounded exemption (EO 6914) by a further explicit bounded term ('until April 1, 1937').

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Single named individual, single bounded term, clear predecessor cited.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Explicit, bounded end date requiring affirmative renewal.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Statutory retirement-age safeguard resumes at the stated date absent further action.

title: Executive Order 7309—Withdrawal of Public Land in Aid of Legislation, Alaska

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, tract-specific temporary withdrawal pending anticipated Congressional action; no ongoing operational discretion is created beyond holding status quo.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Single responsible agency (Interior), narrow scope.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: This is explicitly a 'withdrawal... in aid of legislation' — meaning its own stated purpose is to hold land status pending a specific anticipated legislative action, which is exactly the kind of authority that should track resolution of the condition that justified it. Yet the order provides no mechanism tying its termination to that legislative action (no bill identified, no review date), only the generic 'continues... unless and until revoked.' This matches this project's own previously-named 'temporary withdrawal, no defined end' pattern (EO 7329, EO 8344, EO 7623) — this appears to be an earlier (Feb. 1936) instance of that same recurring gap.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 7310—Exemption of W. H. Moran from Compulsory Retirement for Age

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, individually-named exemption extending a prior bounded exemption (EO 6647) by a further explicit bounded term ('until January 1, 1937'); notably Chief of the Secret Service Division, a sensitive position, but the exemption mechanism itself is identically bounded and renewal-dependent as the other individual exemptions in this batch.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Single named individual, single bounded term, clear predecessor cited.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Explicit, bounded end date requiring affirmative renewal.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Statutory retirement-age safeguard resumes at the stated date absent further action.

title: Executive Order 7311—Partial Revocation of Executive Order No. 6964, of February 5, 1935, Withdrawing Public Lands in Certain States

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow reclassification: releases specific Arkansas tracts from a general classification-pending withdrawal because they have now been affirmatively classified as 'chiefly valuable for recreational use' under the 1926 Act. No new discretionary authority is created.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear basis and responsible agency; no diffusion.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed; the lands move from a generic classification-pending status into a specific, statutorily-defined recreational-use classification.

title: Executive Order 7312—Amendment of Executive Order No. 6928 of December 24, 1934

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Objective, formula-based currency-loss reimbursement rule for employees abroad; no discretionary authority is concentrated anywhere.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, formula-driven entitlement leaves no accountability gap.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: The conversion/reimbursement formula, while technically dense, is fully specified with no discretionary gaps.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: No perverse incentive is identifiable in a currency-loss reimbursement formula.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Routine technical amendment to a standing pay regulation (EO 6928), revised through ordinary amendment as this order itself demonstrates.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

flags.name: Third-Party Incentive Gaps
flags.justification: Affected employees have direct personal access to and interest in their own pay records, substituting for an external watchdog.

title: Executive Order 7313—Revoking Executive Order of July 26, 1905, and Excluding Certain Tracts of Land from Tongass National Forest and Restoring Them to Entry, Alaska

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, tract-specific revocation and restoration-to-entry action, issued 'upon the recommendation of the Secretary of Agriculture' — an explicit endorsement requirement built into the order itself.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear basis and named recommending official.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: The Agriculture Secretary's recommendation is a real check on record, not a unilateral action; no existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 7314—Exemption of Miss Mary G. Wilmarth from Compulsory Retirement for Age

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, individually-named, explicitly time-bound exemption ('until May 1, 1937').

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Single named individual, single bounded term.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Bounded, explicit end date requiring affirmative renewal.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Statutory retirement-age safeguard resumes at the stated date absent further action.

title: Executive Order 7315—Exemption of William Gerig from Compulsory Retirement for Age

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, individually-named, explicitly time-bound exemption ('until April 1, 1937').

flags.name: Accountability Gaps

title: Executive Order 7321—Authorizing Issuance of Temporary Regulations Governing Annual and Sick Leave

flags.name: Power Concentration

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Each department head is individually responsible for its own interim rules; the named statutes create the ultimate accountability point (uniform regulations to come).

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: Because dozens of different department heads may each issue their own temporary leave rules under only the loose constraint 'not inconsistent with law,' federal employees could see materially different leave treatment agency-to-agency during the interim period — a real, if temporary and statutorily anticipated, inconsistency.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Explicitly and specifically temporary: the order's own text ties termination to a concrete, named event ('pending the promulgation of uniform regulations as provided for by the said acts') rather than the vaguer 'in aid of legislation' language in EO 7309 earlier in this batch — a stronger, more clearly defined sunset trigger.

title: Executive Order 7323—Creating a Committee to Complete the Summary of the Results and Accomplishments of the National Recovery Administration and Report Thereon

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A multi-member committee (Secretaries of Commerce, Agriculture, and Labor, plus specially appointed members) with an explicit, textually-stated proviso limiting its scope ('nothing in this Order shall be construed to authorize the Committee... to collect from the general public current statistical information, or to duplicate the statistical work now being performed'). Distributed composition plus an explicit self-limiting clause, similar in kind to the clean bounded-advisory-body pattern already named in this project's findings log.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, multi-member composition with an explicit scope-limiting proviso reduces ambiguity about what the Committee can and cannot do.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: Sec. 5(a) authorizes the Committee to 'appoint and fix the compensation of a Chief of the Division of Industrial Economics and other necessary officers and employees without regard to the Civil Service laws or the Classification Act of 1923' — the same institutional-scale bypass mechanism identified in EO 7305 earlier in this batch, though here bounded to a fixed $100,000 transferred sum and a temporary wind-down function (completing a report on the already-terminated NRA), which meaningfully narrows its scale relative to EO 7305's open-ended nationwide lending-program staff.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Unlike EO 7305's stated calendar date, this Committee's authority is bounded by a fixed, finite transferred sum ($100,000) and an explicitly terminal task (completing a summary report on an already-ended program) rather than an open-ended ongoing function — a real, if implicit, natural end-state.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight mechanism is removed.

flags.name: Third-Party Incentive Gaps
flags.justification: No external party is named with standing to review the Committee's civil-service-bypass hiring and compensation decisions, the same gap identified in EO 7305, though at smaller scale here.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Draws on Commerce, Agriculture, and Labor jointly, and explicitly inherits property/employees transferred from the former NRA under EO 7252; the coordination (shared committee, consensual use of Commerce Department administrative services) is cooperative, not conflicting.

flags.name: Power Concentration

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization

title: Executive Order 7342—Authorizing Initial Appointments to Certain Executive Positions in the Railroad Retirement Board Without Compliance with the Civil Service Act and Rules

title: Executive Order 7347—Amending Executive Order No. 7083 of June 24, 1935, Prescribing Rules and Regulations Relating to Methods of Prosecuting Projects Under the Emergency Relief Appropriation Act of 1935

flags.justification: Diff-aware comparison against EO 7083's baseline: this lowers the minimum self-performance requirement for housing-project foundation contracts from 25% to 15%, meaning up to 85% (versus 75% before) of the covered work may now be subcontracted out. A self-performance floor exists specifically to keep the prime contractor directly accountable for construction quality and compliance; lowering it measurably diffuses that accountability across more subcontracting parties with less direct contractual responsibility to the government.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: The percentage figures themselves are precise and unambiguous.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)

flags.justification: This adjusts a substantive contracting ratio rather than removing a judicial-review, reporting, or administrative-review mechanism of the kind Flag 7 targets; the diffusion-of-accountability concern is captured under Flag 2 instead, per this project's guidance against firing multiple flags off one textual feature without each flag's own test being independently satisfied.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

flags.name: Third-Party Incentive Gaps
flags.justification: Interior itself has direct interest in and access to enforcing the removal condition when the reservoir land is actually needed, substituting for an external watchdog.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Implicates Interior and the independent Federal Power Commission; the explicit removal condition and the housekeeping revocation of the stale prior authorization make this a clean, well-coordinated arrangement.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight

flags.justification: Same textual basis as Flag 5.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Explicitly states it is 'not intended to release the above-described land from the reservation... for any purpose other than the use specified' — a genuinely protective, narrow-construction design feature.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Implicates the Alaska Road Commission's airport use alongside the original 1923 townsite reservation; the explicit reversion language makes this a clean, protective coordination.

title: Executive Order 7364—Enlarging Charles Sheldon Wildlife Refuge in Nevada

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Single responsible agency, narrow single-purpose enlargement.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Evergreen wildlife-conservation designation, consistent with this batch's other refuge orders.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Subject to valid existing rights.

title: Executive Order 7365—Dissolution of Second Export-Import Bank of Washington, D.C.

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Implements a specific statutory dissolution mechanism (1935 Act sec. 9) with a defined winding-up process; the stockholders themselves carry out liquidation under D.C. corporate law, not a discretionary presidential process.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, defined winding-up responsibility, with proceeds explicitly directed to the Treasury as miscellaneous receipts.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: This is itself an orderly institutional wind-down under statute, not a removal of any existing check — a clean, well-defined dissolution comparable in spirit to the self-terminating advisory-body pattern already named in this project's findings log, here applied to a full banking corporation rather than a study committee.

title: Executive Order 7366—Authorizing Initial Appointments to Certain Positions in the Social Security Board Without Compliance With the Civil Service Act and Rules

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Second confirmed instance in this project of the 'bootstrapping a new agency' civil-service-bypass variant identified at EO 7342 (Railroad Retirement Board): bounded to a specific list of named field-organization positions needed to establish the brand-new Social Security Board, explicitly framed as 'initial appointments' only, and requiring each proposed appointee to establish qualifications before the Civil Service Commission.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: The Civil Service Commission qualifications-verification step, plus the Social Security Board's own on-record recommendation, gives a real accountability structure.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: Same mitigating features as EO 7342: bounded, one-time bootstrapping frame plus independent qualifications verification meaningfully distinguish this from the unmitigated individual-bypass pattern found elsewhere in this batch.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: The 'initial appointment' framing supplies a natural end-state, the same reasoning as EO 7342.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: The Civil Service Commission verification step is an added check, not a removed one.

title: Executive Order 7367—Authorizing the Appointment of Mrs. Eva Sweeney to a Classified Position in the Department of Agriculture Without Regard to the Civil Service Act and Rules

flags.name: Power Concentration

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Single named position, clear compassionate basis stated.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: Seventh confirmed instance in this project of the direct civil-service-bypass language ('without compliance with the requirements of the Civil Service Act and Rules') with no CSC qualifications-verification step and no recommendation-of-record, matching the unmitigated pattern of EO 7276/7306/7318/7346/7351.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Administrative jurisdictional designation, evergreen in the same sense as this batch's other jurisdiction-placement orders (EO 7299), notwithstanding the 'continues... unless and until revoked' boilerplate.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 7369—Amendment of Executive Order of January 17, 1873, Relating to the Holding of State or Local Offices by Federal Officers and Employees

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Bounded exception (Indian Service medical/sanitary personnel may hold similar local offices) requiring the Secretary of the Interior's consent, the same structure as EO 7332 earlier in this batch (National Park Service deputy sheriffs).

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear approval chain and explicit conditions (no interference with Indian Service duties; no extra compensation for full-time employees).

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: The conditions are reasonably specific, and Interior Secretary consent gates any ambiguity.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: No perverse incentive identified; explicitly uncompensated for full-time employees and subordinate to primary duties.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Amendment to the same standing 1873 personnel-ethics framework as EO 7332; the order's own text states it 'supersedes Executive Order No. 5188 of September 10, 1929' — direct textual evidence of ordinary-amendment revision.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Adds a bounded, consent-gated exception rather than removing any existing oversight.

flags.name: Exemptions Architecture
flags.justification: A narrow, consent-gated carve-out from a general prohibition, bounded to a specific occupational category and explicit conditions, not broad or self-expanding — the same reasoning as EO 7332.

title: Executive Order 7370—Amendment of Paragraph 10(a), Section IV, Schedule A of Civil Service Rules

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, technical reclassification excluding three specific instructor positions (wrestling, boxing, gymnastics) at West Point from an existing civil-service classification exemption.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, specific scope.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Standing civil-service classification schedule, revised through ordinary amendment.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

flags.name: Exemptions Architecture
flags.justification: This order runs in the opposite direction from this batch's many exemption-granting orders: it removes three specific positions from an existing blanket classification exemption, bringing them under ordinary competitive civil-service coverage instead of expanding an exemption. A civil-service-domain instance of the 'power-narrowing reads clean' pattern already named in this project's findings log for civil-rights contexts (EO 11785, EO 11890).

title: Executive Order 7371—Amendment of Executive Order No. 6160 of June 7, 1933, Prescribing Rules and Regulations Relating to the Administration of the Emergency Conservation Work

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Sets fixed, objective percentage caps (6% and 9% of company strength) and fixed dollar amounts ($45/$36 per month) for CCC merit-pay allowances; the numerical structure itself is bounded, not open discretion.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Objective caps leave clear responsibility for correct administration of the pool sizes.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: The order defers the actual substantive standard for who qualifies — 'grades to be defined by [the Director] for the purpose of rewarding energetic effort and recognizing outstanding leadership' — entirely to future, unspecified regulations, with no criteria given here for what counts as 'energetic effort' or 'outstanding leadership.' Two different company commanders could reach very different views of who merits the allowance.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Tied to Emergency Relief Appropriation Act funding, subject to periodic Congressional reappropriation.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 7372—Amendment of Schedule A of the Civil Service Rules
