flags.justification: Nothing to fail.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Standing classification schedule, revised through ordinary amendment.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 7373—Establishing the Desert Game Range, Nevada

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Establishes joint Interior/Agriculture jurisdiction over wildlife management, with grazing-land authority held by Interior only if the land is separately included in a duly-established grazing district, and migratory-bird-acquired land under Agriculture alone — a deliberately distributed structure, not a concentration, further bounded by an explicit numeric forage-allocation standard (sustaining a maximum of 1,800 Nelson's mountain sheep as the primary species).

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Responsibility is clearly divided by land-use category and use (wildlife: joint; grazing-district land: Interior; migratory-bird land: Agriculture) rather than left ambiguous.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: The management standard is unusually specific for a land order of this era — a numeric population target for the primary species, with an explicit rule that combined wildlife forage consumption may not increase the burden on land dedicated to that primary species.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: No perverse incentive identified; the explicit numeric sustainable-yield standard and multi-layered non-interference design work against, not toward, resource overuse.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Wildlife and range conservation is an evergreen designation, consistent with this batch's other refuge orders.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Explicitly and repeatedly preserves mineral prospecting/mining/leasing rights, prior withdrawals and reservations 'insofar as may be consistent' with their own purposes, and existing valid rights generally — an unusually thorough multi-layered non-interference design for an order of this complexity and scale.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: A genuinely well-designed joint-jurisdiction arrangement between Interior and Agriculture with an explicit division of authority by land-use category — a strong exemplar of the recurring non-interference/clean-coordination pattern in this batch, notable here for its complexity and scale relative to the simpler two-agency cases logged elsewhere (EO 7295, 7302, 7341).

title: Executive Order 7378—Cache National Forest, Utah

flags.justification: The two-official approval chain for any acceleration gives clear, shared responsibility.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: This is the sixth consecutive postponement of the same 1933 government-reorganization provisions (the order's own text names five prior postponements: EO 6224, 6540, 6727, 6927, 7077, 7261, spanning 1933-1935), now pushed to December 31, 1936, with no indication of a final resolution point. A reorganization plan repeatedly deferred rather than either completed or formally abandoned is exactly the kind of authority that should track toward resolution and, after three years and six extensions, structurally does not.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: This is a scheduling deferral, not a removal of any existing review or oversight mechanism.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Distinct from the Flag 6 finding (which concerns the postponement mechanism's own lack of an end-point): the affected agencies have now operated for three years under a reorganization plan whose consolidations and eliminations remain permanently pending, creating ongoing institutional uncertainty about which functions will ultimately be transferred or eliminated — a real, lingering downstream effect of the repeated-deferral pattern itself, worth watching for whether it is ever resolved or becomes a permanent fixture, in the same spirit as this project's Zombie Emergency Trap tracking.

flags.justification: This order is a full incorporation-by-reference of the entire prior ERA-1935 rule corpus for continued application under ERA 1936, per this project's incorporation-by-reference coding convention (an extending order inherits the flag findings of what it wholesale-incorporates). Several of the incorporated components were independently found in this same batch to carry real structural weight — most notably EO 7296 (35.71%, the WPA Administrator's unilateral, vaguely-standardized authority to compel unpaid makeup labor). This record scores only this order's own new facial text; a complete assessment of ERA-1936-era WPA operations should carry those incorporated findings forward rather than treating this bridging order as a fresh, standalone clean baseline.

title: Executive Order 7404—Amendment of Subdivision XI, Schedule B, Civil Service Rules

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Creates a standing, open-ended Schedule B exemption for all future Navy Department civilian employment outside the continental United States (except the Canal Zone and Alaska), triggered solely by 'the Secretary of the Navy's' own subjective judgment that 'the best interests of the service so require' — no external check or defined criteria bounds that judgment.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Because the Secretary of the Navy both sets and applies the 'best interests of the service' standard, there is no separate mechanism for holding that judgment accountable if it is invoked more broadly than the underlying practical necessity (impracticability of competitive hiring abroad) actually requires.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'Best interests of the service' is an undefined standard; two different Secretaries — or the same Secretary at different times — could reach substantially different conclusions about identical overseas hiring situations.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Unlike the well-bounded Schedule A additions elsewhere in this batch (EO 7370's specific instructor exclusions, EO 7393's single named position, EO 7400's Commission-gated veterinarian category), this creates an indefinite, open-ended exemption category with no defined limiting principle or review point — closer in character to the unmitigated individual civil-service-bypass pattern than to this batch's other, narrower Schedule additions.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: This is a new exemption category, not a removal of a pre-existing oversight mechanism.

flags.name: Exemptions Architecture
flags.justification: A broad, open-ended carve-out from competitive civil service for an entire, geographically-defined and indefinite category of future hires, gated only by the appointing Secretary's own unbounded discretion — exactly the combination (broad, discretionary, concentrated in a single actor with no external check) the flag's operational test targets.

title: Executive Order 7409—Prescribing Regulations Relating to Annual Leave of Government Employees

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Implementation authority is decentralized across every department and independent-establishment head (Sec. 18), each administering objective accrual/day-count rules within their own jurisdiction, rather than concentrated in one official — the same distributed structure as EO 7321 (the interim leave order this regulation formally supersedes).

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Sec. 18 explicitly assigns 'heads or governing bodies of the various governmental agencies' responsibility for proper administration.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: Sec. 8's 'leave shall be granted at such time or times as may be deemed to be in the public interest' is a genuinely undefined standard for when accrued leave may actually be taken, distinct from the numerically precise accrual and charging rules that make up the bulk of the regulation; two supervisors could reach very different conclusions about identical leave requests.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: A permanent, general-applicability personnel regulation implementing a permanent 1936 statute (not an emergency/ERA-Act measure); the order's own text explicitly ratifies and supersedes the prior interim department-level regulations issued under EO 7321 — direct diff-aware evidence of ordinary revision practice.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Replaces a patchwork of interim, department-by-department temporary rules (EO 7321) with one uniform standard — if anything this improves consistency rather than removing any existing check.

flags.name: Third-Party Incentive Gaps
flags.justification: Affected employees have direct interest in and access to their own leave records.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Applies uniformly 'to all civilian officers and employees of the United States, the District of Columbia, and all corporations' within scope; a shared standard, not a jurisdictional conflict, with a specific, well-defined WPA carve-out (Sec. 19(e)) rather than an ambiguous one.

flags.name: Exemptions Architecture
flags.justification: Part III's exceptions (D.C. teachers/librarians, Panama Canal/Railroad employees, hourly construction workers, Postal employees, WPA security-wage workers, and various intermittent/fee-basis employees) are narrow, enumerated, and definitionally bounded rather than broad or subject to open-ended official discretion — a well-specified exemptions list under this project's three-part Flag 11 test.

flags.name: Power Concentration

flags.name: Accountability Gaps

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: Worth a direct contrast with EO 7409 (issued the same day): where EO 7409's leave-timing discretion ('deemed to be in the public interest') is genuinely undefined, this sick-leave regulation's comparable discretionary point — advancing leave beyond accumulated balance in cases of 'serious disability... and when the exigencies of the situation so require' (Sec. 3) — is bounded by explicit numeric caps (no more than 30 days advance, no more than 3 times per calendar year) and a mandatory physician's-certificate requirement, making it meaningfully more objective than its sibling order's comparable clause.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives

title: Executive Order 7497—Amending the Instructions to Diplomatic Officers and the Consular Regulations

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Vests the Secretary of State with entirely standardless discretion over whether to authorize a Foreign Service officer's marriage to a foreign national ('under such instructions as may be issued by him') — no criteria, factors, or process are specified anywhere in the text for how this authorization decision is made.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: No review process, appeal mechanism, or timeline is specified for the Secretary's marriage-authorization decision.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: The standard triggering automatic career-ending consequences ('deemed guilty of insubordination and shall be separated from the Service') is entirely undefined; officers must additionally submit a resignation with every authorization request 'for such action as may be deemed appropriate,' compounding the uncertainty about what disposition follows even a compliant request.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: The pairing of an undefined authorization standard with a requirement to submit one's resignation alongside every marriage-authorization request creates a plausible incentive for officers to conceal marriages or relationships from the Department rather than trigger this uncertain, career-jeopardizing process.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: A standing personnel regulation for the Foreign Service, not an emergency or crisis-tied grant of authority requiring sunset review.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: The automatic 'insubordination'/separation trigger for an unauthorized marriage operates as a self-executing disciplinary mechanism with no stated review, appeal, or board process — preempting the kind of procedural check that would ordinarily attach to a personnel-separation action.

flags.name: Third-Party Incentive Gaps
flags.justification: The affected officer has no stated avenue to contest the Secretary's authorization decision or an 'insubordination' determination.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: A broad, intrusive personnel policy reaching every Foreign Service officer's personal marriage decisions, with a plausible chilling effect on relationships with foreign nationals across the entire Service.

title: Executive Order 7498—Authorizing the Alaska Railroad To Engage in Ocean-Going and Coastwise Transportation

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Bounded to a stated real-world necessity (interruption of ocean-going/coastwise shipping to Alaska) and constrained by an explicit rate freeze ('present commercial passenger and freight charges shall be maintained') that limits the practical scope of the authorization.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: The rate-freeze constraint is an explicit, checkable limit on how the authorized authority may be exercised.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'For such period as the Secretary of the Interior may deem necessary' supplies no criteria for when the underlying shipping interruption is considered resolved.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: Zombie Emergency Trap structure: the Secretary of the Interior, who administers the Alaska Railroad's expanded operating authority, is also the sole judge of when the shipping interruption justifying that authority has ended, with no external check or defined end-date tying the two together.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: No defined termination date or trigger; continuation rests entirely on the same official's own ongoing assessment.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

flags.name: Third-Party Incentive Gaps
flags.justification: The explicit rate-freeze already protects shippers/passengers from the main third-party harm (price gouging) that might otherwise accompany this kind of temporary monopoly-adjacent authorization.

title: Executive Order 7526—Postponement of Effective Date of Certain Provisions of Executive Order No. 6166 of June 10, 1933

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: The interim operative-order mechanism is a real, checkable process, slightly more structured than EO 7390's bare deferral.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Matches EO 7390's finding: the deferral mechanism itself has no natural end-point, now in its seventh installment across more than three years, with no resolution in sight.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed; the interim-approval mechanism if anything adds a checkpoint.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Matches EO 7390's finding: continuing, now multi-year, institutional uncertainty for the agencies whose consolidation or elimination remains permanently pending.
